doctrine.aliases: version-unpinned. sonnet/opus/fable/haiku are platform aliases; the platform resolves each to the provider-recommended version, and resolution can legitimately differ by provider, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL, CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL, availableModels allowlists, org restrictions, and modelOverrides. Kiln never resolves a Claude alias and never claims which concrete version will serve; it records the RESOLVED identity per run from the modelUsage keys of the archived stream-json result events. gpt-sol names the codex transport model via the resolver below — codex -m requires a concrete id, so the one versioned string in the product lives there and nowhere else.
doctrine.degradation: no silent model substitution and no fallback rung exists: a broken or unavailable codex transport is the exit-20 honest halt (the crash-broken-codex ruling). Any future fallback enters as a fully-specified doctrine change — trigger class, attempt count, actual-model record, receipt location — never as a bare resolver entry.
doctrine.xhigh: permitted only for extremely-complex, all-layers, global-vision work, selected deliberately per artifact — never required, never a standing default (INTAKE-14b; CONSTITUTION.md:66 'xhigh only by deliberate selection; never ladder tiers on one artifact').

driver.note: the operator's session model — Kiln never overrides it

kernel-leg.note: hands/idsFetch: model-backed, so HIGH (Q1 ruling: a model-backed role defaults HIGH; 'mechanical' means NO model). The truly-mechanical alternative — replacing these legs with deterministic harness execution, no model call at all — is a named SHIP-era option, not built in this slice.

stage-card.note: law/validate/report card agents — the `act` default (kernel.js:188). The build SLICE leg (build + repair) is NOT stage-card: the kernel surface-routes it to builder-ui/builder-logic before agent() (T-03', §2.3). RAISED medium -> high per INTAKE-14 default (kernel.js:188 was the flagged gap)

builder-ui.note: CONSUMED via surface_routing at the KERNEL's builder-leg dispatch (T-03'): the kernel selects the role from the slice's surface BEFORE agent(), never the already-dispatched card. Live routing data, not a reserved key

builder-logic.note: PARKED, not routed (2026-07-19 ruling): the kernel builder leg dispatches through the workflow agent() spawner, which reaches only the Anthropic API — a gpt id there is a 404 model_not_found (cleanroom receipt req_011CdBugiPWMovjbvkCV1L9c), and no codex builder transport exists yet (only the reviewer gate, scripts/kiln-review, speaks codex). Boot validation rejects any gpt-family surface-route target, and logic routes to builder-ui until a codex builder transport lands beside kiln-review; this entry stays declared as that transport's landing pad.

reviewer-gate.note: the cross-family terminal gate (scripts/kiln-review transport). INTAKE-14 default HIGH

brainstorm-facilitator.note: Da Vinci — inherits the session model (agents/da-vinci.md has no model key; stays that way; documented default is inherit per the sub-agents contract)

haiku-migration.note: STRICTLY the explicit haiku-class migration seat (INTAKE-14c: haiku -> sonnet, medium). Never a justification for LOW on any model-backed role; a truly mechanical job takes no model, not a cheap one. No rework consumer today — the landing pad for any surviving haiku-class seat.

dev-sol.note: dev-process Sol reviews (scripts/dev/sol-review.sh defaults already match); xhigh deliberate-only per HANDOFF.md:56
